2015-07-25.abstract: The strikes, which followed Turkish attacks on Islamic State targets in Syria, effectively ended a two-year cease-fire between Turkey and the Kurdistan Workers’ Party.

2015-07-26.title: Migrants Surge Into Europe Through the Balkans
2015-07-26.abstract: Much of Europe is reeling from the flow of people seeking safety, jobs and a better life.

2015-07-26.title: Obama Delivers Tough-Love Message to End Kenya Trip
2015-07-26.abstract: In a speech on Sunday, President Obama challenged the land of his father to end corruption, overcome ethnic divisions and stop discrimination against women.

2015-07-26.title: 1940: Hungary Expects to Get Only Part of Transylvania
2015-07-26.abstract: From the International Herald Tribune archives: Hungary’s aspirations to get Transylvania back are challenged in 1940.

2015-07-26.title: Assad, in Rare Admission, Says Syria’s Army Lacks Manpower
2015-07-26.abstract: President Bashar al-Assad also said that the army had ceded some areas to insurgents in order to hold onto other regions deemed more important.

2015-07-26.title: Obama in Kenya Urges Reform
2015-07-26.abstract: President Obama gave a speech to mostly young Kenyans in Nairobi on Sunday.

2015-07-27.title: Obama’s Visit to Ethiopia Promises Little for Human Rights Activists
2015-07-27.abstract: During President Obama’s visit to the country, the first by a sitting American president, issues like investment and terrorism are expected to take precedence.

2015-07-27.title: Q. and A.: Chen Zhonglin on ‘Picking Quarrels’ Online
2015-07-27.abstract: Mr. Chen, a law professor at Chongqing University, discussed the meaning of “rule of law” and the uses and abuses of the charge of “picking quarrels and provoking trouble.”

2015-07-27.title: 被下药，被绑架，被奴隶
2015-07-27.abstract: 2011年，Asorasak Thamma从泰国被绑架并被迫到一艘渔船上做奴隶。他已经两年没有回家了。

2015-07-27.title: 读者评论区：揭秘南海渔船海上奴工的悲惨生活
2015-07-27.abstract: 南海渔船:生病的渔工被抛弃,不听话的被砍头。

2015-07-27.title: ‘Sea Slaves’: The Human Misery That Feeds Pets and Livestock
2015-07-27.abstract: Men who have fled servitude on fishing boats recount beatings and worse as nets are cast for the catch that will become pet food and livestock feed.

2015-07-28.title: Muslim Image-Makers, Made in Moscow
2015-07-28.abstract: In Russia, some Muslims want to create a more positive image of Islam. A fashion designer, a public relations adviser and a composer all work toward the same goal.

2015-07-28.title: Muslims in Moscow Work to Break a Stereotype
2015-07-28.abstract: As the political landscape shifts in Russia, Muslims, who were once frequently stigmatized, see an opportunity to redefine their role in society.

2015-07-28.title: Lack of Pause in Yemen War Delays Aid
2015-07-28.abstract: Saudi Arabia had promised to cease fighting so that groups could deliver aid to millions of starving Yemenis, but there was no indication that would happen on Monday as planned.

2015-07-28.title: South Korea: Government Declares End to MERS Outbreak
2015-07-28.abstract: South Korea on Tuesday declared a de facto end to an outbreak of Middle East respiratory syndrome that has killed 26 people.

2015-07-28.title: Новый Образ Ислама, Made in Moscow
2015-07-28.abstract: Пасмурным днем в саду четырехзвëздочного отеля на Тверском бульваре собрались девушки в ярких хиджабах и платьях с цветочными принтами.

2015-07-29.title: Top American Commander Warns U.N. That Too Few Carry Efforts for Peace
2015-07-29.abstract: The top American military commander urged United Nations member states on Tuesday to increase their commitments to its global peacekeeping forces.

2015-07-29.title: Brazil Arrests Nuclear Chief in Widening of Graft Case
2015-07-29.abstract: Othon Luiz Pinheiro da Silva is accused of taking more than $1.3 million in bribes as chief executive of Eletronuclear, a company that operates nuclear plants.

2015-07-29.title: Hundreds of Migrants Try to Cross English Channel on Freight Trains
2015-07-29.abstract: Group after group of migrants made their way to the Eurotunnel compound in Calais overnight on Monday, climbing over fences and other barriers in a desperate bid to reach Britain.

2015-07-29.title: U.N. Council to Vote on Creating Tribunal On Downing of Malaysia Airlines Flight
2015-07-29.abstract: The jetliner is believed to have been destroyed by a surface-to-air missile fired from territory controlled by pro-Russia separatists in eastern Ukraine

2015-07-29.title: Samuel Pisar Dies at 86; Lawyer and Adviser Survived Nazi Camps
2015-07-29.abstract: Mr. Pisar survived the Holocaust to create a life of accomplishment, including becoming an adviser to John F. Kennedy, whom he met at Harvard, and as a lawyer to corporate executives and movie stars.

2015-07-30.title: New Ebola Cases Decline, but W.H.O. Advises Caution
2015-07-30.abstract: Seven new cases of Ebola were reported in Guinea and Sierra Leone last week, the lowest weekly total in more than a year.

2015-07-30.title: Finding on Whether Debris Is From Malaysia Airlines Flight 370 May Take a Week
2015-07-30.abstract: The object found on the island of Réunion in the Indian Ocean will be shipped to France, eventually arriving at an aviation laboratory in Toulouse for analysis.

2015-07-30.title: Deals Flow to Contractor Tied to Mexican President
2015-07-30.abstract: Several recent scandals have tainted the legacy of President Enrique Peña Nieto and further eroded the public’s faith in the country’s governing institutions.

2015-07-30.title: Another Tiananmen Square Pops Up Ahead of Military Parade
2015-07-30.abstract: Tight security surrounds the rehearsal grounds for a military parade, as nearby restaurants sit empty and factories are ordered closed.

2015-07-30.title: Ai Weiwei Disputes Restricted Visa From Britain
2015-07-30.abstract: The artist, who was detained for 81 days in 2011, disagrees with British officials over whether he has a criminal conviction and says the visa he received may keep him from attending his installation in London.

2015-07-31.title: Liberia: Ebola Stalls Birth Registrations
2015-07-31.abstract: The births of more than 70,000 children in Liberia during the Ebola crisis were never recorded, leaving them vulnerable to marginalization.

2015-07-31.title: Mullah Muhammad Omar’s Life Ends With Little Clarity
2015-07-31.abstract: Mullah Muhammad Omar, the secretive leader of the Taliban, spent the final years of his life in remarkable obscurity, and his death was confirmed only by chatter among Taliban officials.

2015-07-31.title: Attack on Gay Pride Parade Shakes Israel’s Self-Image as Bastion of Tolerance
2015-07-31.abstract: An Israeli’s violent rampage at the event in Jerusalem was quickly politicized by critics of Israel.

2015-07-31.title: Jewish Arsonists Suspected in West Bank Attack That Killed Palestinian Toddler
2015-07-31.abstract: The attack was branded as terrorism by Israeli and Palestinian politicians, and shocked consciences on both sides of the simmering conflict.

2015-07-31.title: A Nighttime Police Visit in China
2015-07-31.abstract: On the night of July 11, the police in Guangzhou came for the human rights lawyer Ge Wenxiu. They threatened to break down his door.
